content:
Welcome back to the final meeting for the week, completing the daily review for the 2022 ICT Free
Mentorship on YouTube series. The market has finally made a run below the May 12, 2022 low. We will now
direct our attention to the hourly chart.

section_title: MARKET CONSOLIDATION AND KEY TURNING POINT
content:
The market consolidated, then ran up and took the stop on my final balance for that paper trade. It moved
up into a deeper Fair Value Gap, creating the high of the day. The market then broke down below the 3915
and a quarter level. For me, this was the required turning point; I didn't want to engage in any activity prior
to this run.

section_title: TWITTER COMMENTARY AND SON'S TRADING PROGRESS
content:
I provided a commentary on Twitter Spaces between 8:30 and 10:00, discussing psychological, personal,
and trading aspects. While most of my children aren't interested in trading, one of my sons is actively
pursuing it. With his permission, I'm showing his live account. His previous balance, after commissions, was
9,751.66. Today, he added another 3,700, bringing his balance to 13,451.66. This represents approximately
a 300 percent return in 3 weeks, potentially earning him the number one spot on the Robins leaderboard in
less than a month.

section_title: LEVERAGE, REALISTIC EXPECTATIONS, AND MENTORSHIP APPROACH
content:
AMP allows for a higher amount of leverage compared to Robins. Such a return is not typical, and I am not
promising anyone similar results. My son has significant experience from sitting next to me. When we look
at charts together, I lean on my experience and ask him what he sees, similar to how I train all of you. I'll
prompt him by asking, 'What do you see here?' and 'What do you see there?' This process builds his
understanding as the charts develop, and he independently makes decisions to enter or exit trades.

section_title: IDENTIFYING THE KEY SWING LOW BREAK
content:
Today, I was utilizing a specific Swing Low. I wanted to see that break. During my Twitter commentary, I
wasn't interested in anything until that low was taken out, which I discussed. I was giving the market an
opportunity to show willingness to run the highs, which could have happened, but it broke the 3915 and a
quarter level instead. I'll explain how I would have personally been involved and how these ideas helped
propel my son's account today.

section_title: MARKET DELIVERY AND LOWER TIMEFRAME ANALYSIS
content:
The market traded below the short-term low, taking out some sell side, and ultimately took out the 3855
level quite handsomely. We will examine all this activity when we drop down to lower timeframes, starting
with the 15-minute timeframe.

- FAIR VALUE GAPS AND TRADING MODEL FLEXIBILITY
On the 15-minute chart, we see the Fair Value Gap it worked up into. I wasn't interested in the back-and
- forth chopping action. When the 3915 and a quarter level broke, there was a gap that, on lower timeframes,
was in my opinion, the better trade. Some might argue it didn't go to a premium, but I am teaching you how
to find the highest probability trades and also how to trust the model. I am not limited to just one model.

I understand some of you want me to provide daily examples of the model, but that takes away your
discovery process. You need to go into your charts and find them yourself. Building a backlog of old data
showing these types of moves helps with your confidence, builds your own pattern recognition, and that's
just the way you are supposed to do it. I'm not going to do it for you or hold your hand. I've taught you what
it looks like, where it forms, so you can go through your charts, look for it to form, and collect examples.
That's backtesting.

section_title: AFTERNOON SESSION PRICE ACTION AND SON'S TRADES
content:
The market runs through 372 and a quarter, creating another opportunity with a visible Fair Value Gap,
leading to another short that worked lower, finally creating the short-term low. At 1:30 PM, we start
watching the afternoon PM session. The market rallied up, created a short-term high, and formed a Fair
Value Gap in the last hour of trading. It rallied back up into a Fair Value Gap, reaching a premium in that
range, which is a target as I've taught. This represents an imbalance, a Fair Value Gap reversal on Friday,
trading up into an old imbalance. My son participated in shorts in this area, taking two shorts, and also
went long in this area, successfully pushing his account up by 3,700 today.

- PM SESSION AND ALGORITHMIC SPOOL
On the five-minute chart, the same idea unfolds. After creating the low at 1:30 PM, the market starts its
business, reaching for the middle of the range, into a premium. This is the array above half the move,
where 50 percent is equilibrium and this is the premium level. The market makes a small drop into the Fair
Value Gap after 3:00 PM, then enters an algorithmic spool, where price action runs aggressively into the
close, hitting a completely random level shown on the hourly chart, before consolidating into the close.

section_title: FAIR VALUE GAPS VS. TRADITIONAL SUPPORT AND RESISTANCE
content:
Let's focus on this specific area. Here's a Fair Value Gap: the market rallies up into it, then breaks lower.
There's another Fair Value Gap; the market trades up into it and sells off. Notice these occurrences are at
the levels I've marked on the chart old lows where sell side liquidity would be resting. We're using the Fair
Value Gap after a run below, akin to broken support, returning back into the Fair Value Gap. This is NOT
'go back to the old low broken and act as resistance.' If the Fair Value Gap doesn't exist, I don't trust that
level as old support broken turned resistance. I classify specific levels beforehand, looking at them with the
logic that the market will create these patterns around them. I'm not trading break and retest or simple
support and resistance.

section_title: UNDERSTANDING IMBALANCE AND CONFLUENCE IN TRADES
content:
Consider this: an old low with a Fair Value Gap. The market trades up into it. Why didn't it just stop at the
3872 and a quarter level? Because it's going into the imbalance here, and then it delivers lower. My son's
two shorts were in here and here, and his longs were in here and here, riding up into the imbalance. It's
difficult to argue against a live account proving these concepts, especially when it's your own son executing
the trades. That's a truly good feeling as a dad.

section_title: IMPORTANCE OF SPECIFIC LEVEL BREAKS AND VOLATILITY MANAGEMENT
content:
Hopefully, what I shared this week was insightful, and today I wanted you to understand why I was not
willing to take a trade earlier: I wanted the 3915 and a quarter level to be broken first. This break on the 15
- minute timeframe provides the confirmation. All the prior price action was consolidated. I didn't want to
catch a break below a short-term low only for it to then run relative equal highs. I chose this specific short
- term low because of the current market volatility. I needed to 'know that I know.' I mentioned this in my
commentary on Twitter this morning.

section_title: LIQUIDITY-DRIVEN LEVELS AND CONFLUENCE OF PATTERNS
content:
The gap, which wasn't visible on the 15-minute timeframe but appeared on the 5-minute chart, is what my
son employed as his trade. There were subsequent trade opportunities down here, and here, and here. The
key levels I note are not indiscriminate; they are built on logic and based on liquidity, not merely support
and resistance. These are levels where I know orders are likely to be, based on the logic that the market is
likely to reach for the 3855 level only if we break below here, and then we have a Fair Value Gap, and then
we break this level here, and does it get another Fair Value Gap? Yes, and then it delivers even more. It's a
matter of all these things coming together as a confluence, not just one single factor leading to the
conclusion or the exercise of pushing the button. It requires a lot more, which is what I was helping you with
yesterday and hopefully this morning.

I'm receiving feedback on Twitter that the content was helpful, and some of you even took live or paper
trades and saw it. This morning, when closing the presentation on Twitter, I was discussing certain levels,
stating, 'I don't have a setup yet, it's sloppy.' This referred to all the consolidation occurring here. As we
approached the 10 o'clock hour, I aimed to be off air because I was anticipating the algorithm taking out
that low. It did, and then it created the pattern I taught you: the Fair Value Gap. We have the displacement,
and I want to see it take off.

section_title: DISPLACEMENT LEG AND LOWER TIMEFRAME PREMIUM
content:
If you look at this displacement leg, you might measure it and say it didn't go to a premium. I don't expect it
to go to a premium here because the market is in a hurry to get down below the 3855 level for the week.
Let's go one more time into the lower timeframe, actually the first time going into the lower timeframe,
scrubbing back to here. Here is that return into the Fair Value Gap. When it does this, I'm looking for it to
move quickly away and start gravitating towards the other pool of liquidity down here.
1
- MINUTE CHART:

Looking at it from the relationship of a short-term dealing range, from this swing high to that swing low, we
have our Fair Value Gap here. It trades up into it at that 3915 and a quarter level. So, it's a confluence of
several things, but the level itself just highlights where the liquidity would be. The imbalance is what I'm
keying off of and focusing on. Equilibrium or above is what constitutes a premium, and that's what this is
here on the one-minute chart.

section_title: THE EFFORT OF LEARNING AND FINAL REMARKS
content:
Don't be discouraged or think the market didn't go to a premium; you just don't have the experience to see
it yet. You have to do this for a while, folks. It isn't a 'watch one time and then you know it' concept; it takes
effort on your part. But I promise you, if you work at it, you're going to get it. And once you have it, you can't
forget it; it's like riding a bicycle. Hopefully, this week has been insightful, and you learned something.
Hopefully, I was able to propel your understanding a little bit beyond what you were expecting. This
concludes my shares for 2022. I have more things to share, but I hope you enjoyed this week with me. I'll
touch base with you next Tuesday, Lord willing. Until then, enjoy your weekend and be safe.
